name: Talking God
description: Talking God, also known as Haashch'ééłti'í, is a prominent and benevolent deity within Navajo mythology. He is one of the Ye'ii, the Holy People, and is often considered a messenger and intercessor between humans and the higher powers. Talking God is frequently depicted as a figure with a white face, a conical hat adorned with feathers, and carrying a white staff. His voice, a high-pitched falsetto, is considered sacred and is imitated during Navajo ceremonies. He is associated with dawn, springtime, and new beginnings, embodying hope and guidance. He assists in healing ceremonies and is believed to bring blessings and protection. Talking God's presence ensures the proper order and harmony within the world, and his role in creation narratives emphasizes his importance. He provides crucial information and assistance to the Hero Twins in their quest to defeat monsters and restore balance to the land. As a benevolent figure, Talking God is sought for his wisdom, healing powers, and ability to connect humans to the divine realm, offering guidance and support in times of need. He is a vital figure in maintaining balance and prosperity within the Navajo universe.
subtitle: Messenger of the Holy People

keyMyths.title: Talking God and the Emergence
keyMyths.description: Talking God guides the people through the various worlds during the Emergence, helping them find their way to the surface world. He provides wisdom and direction during this crucial journey.

keyMyths.title: Talking God and the Hero Twins
keyMyths.description: Talking God assists the Hero Twins in their quest to defeat the monsters threatening the world, providing them with knowledge and weapons. His guidance is essential for their success.

keyMyths.title: Talking God's Healing Power
keyMyths.description: Talking God is invoked in healing ceremonies to restore balance and harmony to the individual and their surroundings. His presence is believed to facilitate the healing process.

sources.author: Berard Haile
